claimText: The Global Plastic Pollution Treaty is being negotiated as an international legally binding instrument that addresses the full lifecycle of plastics.
evidenceExcerpt: The treaty aims for an international legally binding instrument addressing the full plastic lifecycle.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope and legal nature of the Global Plastic Pollution Treaty.
distractorHooks: Addresses only plastic disposal and recycling

claimText: India is part of the 'Like Minded Countries' group that rejects restrictions on plastic production in the Global Plastic Pollution Treaty negotiations.
evidenceExcerpt: Like Minded Countries (including India, China, Saudi Arabia) reject restrictions on plastic production
examUse: Statement-based question on India's official stance in international plastic treaty negotiations.
distractorHooks: India is part of the High Ambition Coalition
India advocates for strict plastic production caps

claimText: MARPOL Annex V is an international regulation that bans the discharge of plastics from ships into the marine environment.
evidenceExcerpt: MARPOL Annex V (1988) banning ship-based plastic discharges
examUse: Correct-pair question matching conventions to their specific environmental mandates.

claimText: The 2019 amendments to the Basel Convention mandate that the transboundary movement of plastic waste is subject to Prior Informed Consent (PIC) rules.
evidenceExcerpt: the 2019 Basel Convention amendments bringing plastic waste under PIC rules
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope of the Basel Convention regarding plastic waste.
distractorHooks: Rotterdam Convention governs plastic waste PIC
Plastic waste is exempt from Basel Convention rules
